content: By 2025, autonomous self-supervised learning agents can evolve an intrinsic 'curiosity gradient' that lets them spontaneously partition their own memory into nested, task-specific sub-agents, turning the single monolithic agent framework into a dynamically self-assembling population of specialized curiosity-driven mini-agents. This emergent self-organization eliminates the need for human-specified task boundaries and lets the system adapt its agent architecture on the fly to complex open-ended environments.
